Added cases get their minimum-viable DDL — an **added FK** → `ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT … FOREIGN KEY` via a **new `Statement.AlterTableAddForeignKey` variant** (ScriptDom `buildAlterTableAddForeignKey` reusing the proven `foreignKeyConstraint`; renders to real T-SQL, asserted); an **added index** → `CREATE INDEX` (reusing `SsdtDdlEmitter.createIndexStatements`, A18-pure — empty overlay, the index's own uniqueness, never a tightening); an **added sequence** → `CreateSequence`; a **Trust-only FK change** → the WITH NOCHECK two-step (`AlterTableDisableConstraint` + `AlterTableNoCheckConstraint`, 6.A.6). Every other change/removal **refuses fail-loud** with a named Error (`migration.destructiveReferenceDrop` / `migration.unsupportedReferenceChange` / `migration.unresolvedReferenceAdd` / `migration.unsupportedIndexChange` / `migration.destructiveIndexDrop` / `migration.unsupportedSequenceChange` / `migration.destructiveSequenceDrop`) — the same add-vs-refuse discipline as the attribute channel, so a refused change becomes a `MigrationError.RefusedBySchemaErrors` (fail-loud), never a silent skip. **Reuse exposed:** `SsdtDdlEmitter.foreignKeyDefOf` (public, over the private `fkDef` + `buildLookups`) and `SsdtDdlEmitter.createIndexStatements`. Deploy order in `emit`: sequences → column adds/alters → indexes → FKs. **New variant's blast radius** (closed-DU discipline): `Statement.fs` (def), `ScriptDomBuild.fs` (builder + `buildStatement` arm), `Deploy.fs` (DDL arm), `DacpacEmitter.fs` (`isSchemaStatement`) — exactly the sites that match the sibling `AlterTableAddColumn`. **Witnesses:** 7 `` ``C1 emit: …`` `` in `SchemaMigrationEmitterTests.fs`. **Remaining reach:** the destructive cases under explicit `--allow-drops` (DROP CONSTRAINT/INDEX/SEQUENCE) + `ALTER SEQUENCE` for a reshaped sequence.

- **B1 — live `migrate --execute` CLI.** `projection migrate --to <modelB.json> --conn <env|file:ref> --execute [--allow-drops] [--allow-cdc]` (`runMigrateExecute`, `Program.fs`) makes Promise 8 operator-reachable: it is **R6-gated** (`PROJECTION_ALLOW_EXECUTE=1` or exit 7, mirroring `transfer --execute`), resolves the connection via `ConnectionResolver.openSubstrate`, runs the **A1 connection pre-flight before any mutation**, then drives `MigrationRun.executeFromLive` (reads the deployed state A live → evolves in place to B → reads B' → reports VERIFIED / refused). Flag-order-independent parse (a general `migrate … --execute` dispatch arm, placed after the exact preview arms). **Shape:** in-place over one `--conn` (the sink at A); the cross-substrate `--source-conn` data-load form maps to `executeWithData` and is a follow-on. **A2 permission pre-flight wiring** into the live path is the survey-gated follow-on (the gate + probe exist in `Preflight`; threading planned-writes + object-scope grant waits on survey P1). No CLI test project exists; B1's gating logic was **smoke-verified without a DB** (R6 → exit 7; missing args → exit 2; read-fail → exit 6; flag-order independence), and the live `executeFromLive` path is covered by the existing Docker `MigrationCanaryTests`.

**Verification.** Full solution builds clean. Fast pure pool: **2665 passed / 207 skipped / 0 failed** (= prior 2658 + 7 emitter witnesses). The new FK-add ScriptDom path is render-asserted. bench/ artifacts from the B1 smoke runs are gitignored.

**Cross-references:** `DEBRIEF_2026_06_02_…` clusters C1 (emitter-side status) + B1 (status); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` §III Wave 6.A.11 + 6.D.1; `src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/SchemaMigrationEmitter.fs` + `Statement.fs` + `ScriptDomBuild.fs` + `SsdtDdlEmitter.fs`; `src/Projection.Cli/Program.fs` (`runMigrateExecute`); `tests/Projection.Tests/SchemaMigrationEmitterTests.fs`. Next frontier: the destructive-under-allow-drops emission, A2 permission wiring + A3 transactional (survey-gated), and the cross-substrate `--source-conn` data-load CLI.

## 2026-06-02 — C1 destructive emission (under --allow-drops) + B1 A2-permission wiring + the cross-substrate data-load CLI

**Resolved (code).** The three follow-ons named in the entry above.

- **C1 destructive emission (under `--allow-drops`).** `SchemaMigrationEmitter.emitWith allowDrops` threads the operator's drop-acceptance through every channel. With `allowDrops`, destructive touches **emit** instead of refusing: attribute Removed → **DROP COLUMN** (`AlterTableDropColumn`); FK Removed → **DROP CONSTRAINT** (`AlterTableDropConstraint`); FK non-trust Changed → DROP CONSTRAINT + ADD (reshape); index Removed → **DROP INDEX** (`DropIndex`); index Changed → DROP INDEX + CREATE INDEX; sequence Removed → **DROP SEQUENCE** (`DropSequence`); sequence Changed → DROP SEQUENCE + CREATE SEQUENCE. Reshape = DROP-then-recreate (value-preserving `ALTER SEQUENCE` is a noted refinement — DROP+CREATE is always faithful to B's end-state, gated by allowDrops). With `allowDrops=false` (the preserved default, `emit = emitWith false`) every drop refuses fail-loud exactly as before. The drop/old-name resolution reads the **source** catalog/kind (the removed element is absent from the target); `MigrationRun.preview` now calls `emitWith allowDrops` so `Migration.plan`'s cleared violations and the emitter agree (previously `migrate --allow-drops` could not proceed past a column/FK/index/sequence drop because the emitter refused regardless). **4 new Statement variants** (`AlterTableDropColumn` / `AlterTableDropConstraint` / `DropIndex` / `DropSequence`), each with a ScriptDom builder (`AlterTableDropTableElementStatement` / `DropIndexStatement` / `DropSequenceStatement`) + the same 4 match sites as `AlterTableAddColumn`. **7 destructive witnesses** (each render-asserted: DROP COLUMN/CONSTRAINT/INDEX/SEQUENCE in the rendered T-SQL; reshape DROP+recreate; the gate-holds-without-the-flag guard).

- **B1 — A2 permission gate wired.** `runMigrateExecute` now reads state A live, **previews to derive the planned writes** (`plannedWritesOf` maps each schema statement → its sink write: ALTER on the table for the ALTER-family + index DDL, CREATE for new tables/sequences), then runs `migratePreflights` (A1 `connectionPreflight` + A2 `captureGrantEvidence` + `permissionPreflight`) **before any mutation**, then `MigrationRun.execute`. A grant the login lacks → `migrate.insufficientGrant` refusal (exit 7) before writing. A2's grant capture is database-scope; object-scope is the survey-gated P1 refinement.

- **The cross-substrate data-load CLI (`runMigrateWithData`).** `projection migrate --to <modelB.json> --sink-conn <ref> --source-conn <ref> --execute [--allow-drops] [--allow-cdc]` evolves the **sink**'s schema A→B in place (reading A live), pre-flights the source (read) + sink (write), then transfers rows from the **source** into the migrated sink over contract B via `MigrationRun.executeWithData` (the data leg runs only if the schema verified). Straight load today (empty reconciliation); the `--reconcile` / `--user-map` re-key forms are the follow-on (the `transfer` verb already carries that machinery). The dispatch routes on the presence of `--source-conn`: present → cross-substrate; absent → in-place `--conn`. Shared CLI helpers extracted: `plannedWritesOf`, `reportMigrationError`, `migratePreflights`.

**Verification.** Full solution builds clean. Fast pure pool: **2672 passed / 207 skipped / 0 failed** (= prior 2665 + 7 destructive witnesses). The new DROP variants are render-asserted. Both live paths (`execute` / `executeWithData`) are covered by the existing Docker `MigrationCanaryTests`; the CLI dispatch (in-place vs cross-substrate, R6 gate, missing-args) is smoke-verified without a DB (exit 7/2). Lint clean of new mutation/sprintf.

**Cross-references:** `DEBRIEF_2026_06_02_…` C1 (destructive status) + B1 (A2 + cross-substrate status); `EXECUTION_PLAN.md` §III Wave 6.A.11 + 6.D.1; `src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/SchemaMigrationEmitter.fs` (`emitWith`) + `Statement.fs` (4 DROP variants) + `ScriptDomBuild.fs`; `src/Projection.Cli/Program.fs` (`runMigrateExecute` / `runMigrateWithData` / `plannedWritesOf` / `migratePreflights`); `tests/Projection.Tests/SchemaMigrationEmitterTests.fs`. Next frontier: value-preserving `ALTER SEQUENCE`, the `--reconcile` re-key in the cross-substrate load, A2 object-scope grants + A3 transactional (survey-gated).

## 2026-06-02 — Clarification: `SchemaMigrationEmitter`'s ALTER/DROP is the imperative-executor lens, NOT the declarative SSDT deploy artifact (operator question)

**Resolved (positioning + one fix).** The operator asked whether the destructive emission (above) is "SSDT-compliant," noting that "these emissions were not necessary given it's a declarative model." Correct on both counts, and consistent with the codebase's own `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` §4 ("the imperative schema ALTER is *not* the deploy artifact"; 6.A.12 "re-seated by function" as a preview/verification lens). The resolution names the two paths explicitly and hardens one classification; the operator chose to **keep the destructive emission as the in-place executor lens** (not pivot to a declared-loss gate, not revert).

- **The two schema-evolution paths.** (1) **Declarative deploy (SSDT-compliant):** `SsdtDdlEmitter` (CREATE TABLE) + `DacpacEmitter` (`.dacpac`) + `.refactorlog` → **DacFx computes the ALTER/DROP at publish** (under `BlockOnPossibleDataLoss` / `DropObjectsNotInSource`); a drop is *absence in the target model*, not hand-emitted DDL. (2) **Imperative `migrate --execute`:** `SchemaMigrationEmitter` → ALTER/DROP → `MigrationRun.execute` runs them directly via `Deploy.executeBatch` against a live connection (no DacFx). The 6.A.12/C1 emitter — additive and destructive — serves **only path (2)**: the live-square T16 executor + the preview/verification lens. It is **not** declarative-SSDT-compliant, and for path (1) the emissions are unnecessary (DacFx owns them). The destructive emission is therefore the *in-place evolver's* drop, distinct from the declarative drop-by-absence-then-DacFx.

- **Decision (operator): keep as the in-place executor lens.** The imperative path is real, tested (the live A→B canary / T16), and useful for evolving a deployed (e.g. empty-PROD / dev / UAT) DB in place without a full DacFx publish cycle. The destructive emission is correct *there* and gated (`--allow-drops` + R6 + connection/permission pre-flights). It stays — but explicitly scoped as the executor/lens, never positioned as the deploy artifact.

- **Fix landed: `DacpacEmitter.isSchemaStatement` no longer admits imperative-migration DDL.** It previously marked the ALTER/DROP-migration family `true`, which would feed a `DROP COLUMN`/`DROP INDEX`/`DROP SEQUENCE` into the declarative `TSqlModel.AddObjects` (a `.dacpac` is desired-state object *definitions* — you cannot `AddObjects` a removal). Latent today (DacpacEmitter is fed the CREATE-only `SsdtDdlEmitter` stream), but the classification was wrong. Now the whole imperative-migration family (`AlterTableAddColumn` / `AlterTableAlterColumn` / `AlterTableAddForeignKey` / `AlterTableDropColumn` / `AlterTableDropConstraint` / `DropIndex` / `DropSequence`) returns `false`; CREATE-family + the declarative post-CREATE state-reproduction alters (`AlterTableNoCheckConstraint` / `AlterTableDisableConstraint` / `AlterIndexDisable`, which the `SsdtDdlEmitter` declarative stream emits) stay `true`. The `SchemaMigrationEmitter` module doc now states the lens positioning verbatim.

**Verification.** Full solution builds clean. Dacpac + SchemaMigration + Migration + MigrationRun tests: 68/68 (the classification change is behavior-preserving — those statements never reached `DacpacEmitter`). **Why the original framing was right and what it missed (carry-forward):** the imperative ALTER is "right by name (it *is* the minimal touch), re-seated by function" — when extending it (e.g. the destructive emission), keep it on the executor side and out of the declarative `.dacpac` / DacFx publish path. A future "declared-loss gate over DacFx publish" (the ontology's stated Remove cell, ⬚ not built) remains the path for *declarative* drops if/when the declarative deploy needs operator-acknowledged data loss.

**Cross-references:** `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` §4 (the DacFx-delegation seam; the repositioning); `src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/DacpacEmitter.fs` (`isSchemaStatement`); `src/Projection.Targets.SSDT/SchemaMigrationEmitter.fs` (module doc — lens positioning); the 2026-06-02 destructive-emission entry above (the work this clarifies).

## 2026-06-03 — The declared-loss gate (Remove cell) + the snapshot⊖snapshot diff loop (closing the latent calculus)

**Built (operator-directed: "build the declared loss gate"; then "wire the snapshot loop now" + "both gates" + the SSDT-forward framing).** Two foundations landed together.

- **Declared-loss gate (P-GATE for the §5 Remove cell).** New `SchemaLoss` DU (`[<RequireQualifiedAccess>]` — `DropIndex`/`DropSequence` collide with `Statement`'s C1 variants) enumerating **every** destructive removal across **all** `CatalogDiff` channels: `DropKind` / `DropAttribute` / `DropReference` / `DropIndex` / `DropSequence`. The prior `violationsOf` covered only kinds + attributes — a silent P-GATE gap (a removed FK/index/sequence was *not* a named violation; it was caught later at the emitter). `Migration.destructiveLosses` is the complete enumeration; `Migration.lossToken` renders the source-free declarable handle (`kind:<k>` / `attr:<k>.<a>` / `fk:` / `index:` / `seq:`, SsKey-root based — P-ID, identity is the match key); `Migration.undeclaredLosses` is the gate. `LossDeclaration` (`DeclareNone` default | `DeclareAll` ≡ prior `allowDrops=true` | `DeclareThese of Set<string>`) replaces the blanket `allowDrops: bool` through `Migration.plan` / `MigrationRun.preview`/`execute`/`executeFromLive`/`executeWithData`. **Granularity is enforced engine-side**: `DeclareThese` clears only the named tokens; any other removal stays undeclared and refuses fail-loud — so the declarative deploy's coarse levers (`DropObjectsNotInSource` / `BlockOnPossibleDataLoss`) are only ever authorized once *every* actual drop is declared. CLI: `--allow-drops` → `DeclareAll`; repeatable `--declare-drop <token>` → `DeclareThese`; refusals print the tokens to copy.

- **Snapshot⊖snapshot diff loop (`MigrationRun.previewFromStore`).** The morphology's "calculus is latent" finding had a precise locus: `record` *writes* an `Episode`, but no production path *read it back* as state A — `plan`/`preview` took authored catalogs; `--execute` read A live. `previewFromStore path declaration target` now sources A from the durable `LifecycleStore` via `reconstructLatestSchema` (the FTC fold, already witnessed durable in `LifecycleStoreTests`), diffing the new emission B against the prior emission's snapshot. **A missing store is genesis** (A = ∅, all Add, no losses). This commits §10's decision-owed #1: *the prior snapshot IS an engine input at emit time.* CLI: `migrate --to B --store <lifecycle.json>` (vs `--from A --to B` two-model).

- **Positioning (per the operator + handbook ch. 4/6).** The gate authorizes the **declarative** SSDT deploy (CREATE + refactorlog + pre/post-deploy scripts; DacFx computes the ALTER/DROP at publish under Octopus — schema first, then the post-deploy data load). The imperative `migrate --execute` ALTER/DROP remains the dev/verification lens (consumes the same `LossDeclaration` coarsely). The data DELETE-scope gate (P-DEL-SCOPE, the data twin) + the CDC-aware minimal-delta MERGE remain the next slice (post-schema, comparison-basis parameterized: prior-snapshot OR target-reality).

**Verification.** Full solution builds clean; pure pool green (0 failed). New witnesses: `destructiveLosses spans the FK channel` (P-GATE completeness — the prior gate missed it); `DeclareThese clears the named loss but refuses an undeclared sibling` (granular gate); `previewFromStore` genesis (all Add) + against the reconstructed prior snapshot (same δ as the two-model preview). The bool→`LossDeclaration` test conversion is behavior-preserving (`DeclareNone`≡false, `DeclareAll`≡true), incl. the Docker `MigrationCanaryTests` (compile-verified).

**Cross-references:** `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md` §5 (Remove cell; the declared-loss gate it named ⬚), §10 (decision-owed #1, now committed); `handbook/04` + `handbook/06` (state-based deploy + pre/post-deploy); `src/Projection.Core/Migration.fs` (`SchemaLoss`/`LossDeclaration`/`destructiveLosses`/`lossToken`/`undeclaredLosses`); `src/Projection.Pipeline/MigrationRun.fs` (`previewFromStore` + threaded executors); `src/Projection.Cli/Program.fs` (`parseLossDeclaration` + `runMigrateFromStore`). Next: data DELETE-scope gate + the CDC-aware minimal-delta MERGE (the data leg of the same slice).

## 2026-06-03 — `THE_USE_CASE_ONTOLOGY.md` is the single index of the target; the prior apexes become provenance

**Decided (operator-directed: "build the masterwork matrix ontology"; then wire it as the index, verify it holes-free, and map the code onto it).** The scattered north-star corpus — five surfaces each claiming or implying apex status (`NORTH_STAR.md`, `PRODUCT_AXIOMS.md`, `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md`, `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md`, `SPINE.md`), plus the WAVE_6 algebra/morphology siblings and `VISION.md` — is now **indexed by a single masterwork**: `THE_USE_CASE_ONTOLOGY.md`. It is **target-first** (the ideal end state in full complexity, not the present build) and carries, in one document: the closed **amino-acid alphabet** (11 structural moves + 10 cross-cutting verbs — the atomic operations of change-over-time), the **protein catalog** (nine operator workflows as ordered amino-acid chains), the **master matrix** (every move × context cell across all ten axes, with the full cell schema: physical realization · substrate constraint · faithfulness class · gate · artifact · measurement · failure prevented · discriminating predicate), the **laws** (the torsor T12–T16/A43, the L1/L2/L3 faithfulness ladder, the intent filter, channel partition, CDC-as-norm minimality, the master equation, provenance replay), a **predicate legend** (§5.0 — every `P-*` code resolved in one place), the **glossary + corpus map** (§6.2 — each prior doc reduced to its provenance role), and a **completeness checklist** (§7).

- **Reading order (amends `DECISIONS 2026-05-22 — CLAUDE.md reading-order update` and its apex amendment).** `THE_USE_CASE_ONTOLOGY.md` is read **first** of the substantive surfaces, above `NORTH_STAR.md`. `NORTH_STAR.md` remains the apex *vision* (now indexed); `VISION.md` remains the cutover-era operational frame; the others are consulted for the depth on the fragment they originated. For the **current code-vs-target distance**, read `DEBRIEF_2026_06_02_ISOMORPHISM_CLIMB_AND_BACKLOG.md` — the masterwork is the target, the debrief is the distance. CLAUDE.md updated.

- **Supersession banners.** A one-line provenance banner was added atop `NORTH_STAR.md`, `WAVE_6_ONTOLOGY.md`, `WAVE_6_ALGEBRA.md`, `WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY.md`, `PRODUCT_AXIOMS.md`, `SPINE.md`, and `AUDIT_2026_05_31_FIVE_AXIS_REDTEAM.md`, each naming the fragment it remains canonical-provenance for. **`PRODUCT_AXIOMS.md` is explicitly NOT superseded** — it remains the live L3 operator-contract registry the masterwork's laws cite.

- **Discipline carried.** Pass one is the **target**, not the present (twice-stated because it is the failure mode); a later pass maps current code onto the matrix to expose the ⬚ gaps. The masterwork is the spine the next thread starts from, standing tall rather than reassembling the map.

**Cross-references:** `THE_USE_CASE_ONTOLOGY.md` (the masterwork; §6.2 the corpus map); `CLAUDE.md` (reading order); the seven bannered provenance docs; `DEBRIEF_2026_06_02_ISOMORPHISM_CLIMB_AND_BACKLOG.md` (the current-state ledger / pass-two surface). Pass two (map HEAD onto every cell, ladder-leveled) and pass three (lab-grade acceptance criteria + fitness evaluation) follow.

## 2026-06-04 — Retire the speculative writer-trinity / optics-duo / Kleisli-product algebra (dead-code deletion)

**Decided (audit-directed; the `AUDIT_2026_06_04_PERIPHERY_ADVERSARIAL.md` §8 trajectory steelman).** Seven algebraic surfaces shipped in the 2026-05-22 Cluster-B burst, each justified by a *symmetry argument* ("completes the duo / trinity / Kleisli category") rather than a consumer. An adversarial trajectory probe established they were **net-negative as shipped**: the two whose intended consumers actually landed both *declined the prebuilt structure* — policy-diff (`PolicyDiff.diffFullProjection`) runs two flat `Lineage` carriers + an `SsKey` join instead of `LineageTree`; the Catalog↔DDL round-trip runs as the deploy→readback canary over `PhysicalSchema` + `Tolerance` instead of a symmetric `Prism`. A down-payment the consumer declines on arrival is stranded inventory. The R&D dividend (mapping the writer-trinity / Kleisli / optics design space) is already fully banked in the docs; the *code* added carrying cost plus a standing false-symmetry temptation (the 2026-06-04 audit's central thesis).

**Retired (zero production callers — grep-verified; `src/Projection.Core/`):**
- `LineageTree<'a>` + `lineageTree { }` CE (`Lineage.fs`, ~294 LOC). Intended consumer (policy-diff, H-033/H-035) shipped on flat-runs-plus-keyed-join.
- `Certificate<'a>` (`Diagnostics.fs`, ~109 LOC). Admitted structural iso with `Lineage<Diagnostics<'a>>` — nominal sugar; rebuild the ~10-LOC record if H-009 multi-target fanout lands.
- `Prism<'a, 'b>` (`Diagnostics.fs` + `Optics.fs`, ~102 LOC). The round-trip it formalized already runs over `PhysicalSchema` (a lossy quotient), not as a symmetric `Get`/`ReverseGet` on `Catalog`.
- `PassContext<'env, 'a>` reader comonad (`Diagnostics.fs`, ~93 LOC). Its own defer-trigger admitted F# parameter-passing is fine at the codebase's depth.
- `Pass.product` / `PassOperators.(&&&)` / `Pass.first` / `Pass.second` (`Diagnostics.fs`, ~35 LOC). The built `product` was sequential anyway; real parallel composition needs the deferred SsKey-disjointness check, built together.
- `DiagnosticRelation` + `DiagnosticLattice.relations` / `isMinimal` / `minimal` (`Diagnostics.fs`). **Kept** `DiagnosticLattice.subsumes` (~17 LOC, the one surface with non-trivial domain content — the dot-separated-`Code` subsumption rule) as a documented design note; rebuild the minimal-set reduction with the operator `diagnose` verb when it lands, likely as rollup-with-counts rather than antichain-deletion.
- `CatalogLenses.sequences` / `CatalogLenses.indexesOf` (`Optics.fs`). Self-flagged "no production consumer yet"; recreate the 5-LOC lens in the commit that adds the first consumer.

**Kept (load-bearing).** `Lineage` / `Diagnostics` / `LineageDiagnostics` writers; the `Pass<'a,'b>` Kleisli arrow with `id` / `compose` / `composeAll` / `>=>` (the live pass-chain composition); `Lens` / `CatalogLenses` (the live lens family: `modules` / `kindsOf` / `attributesOf` / `referencesOf` / `columnOf`).

**Supersedes the load-bearing "Writer-monad trinity" commitment (`DECISIONS 2026-05-22`).** The trinity collapses to the **linear** writer (`Lineage`) plus the stacked `Diagnostics`; the branching (`LineageTree`) and terminal (`Certificate`) members are retired. The "optics duo" (`Lens` + `Prism`) collapses to `Lens` alone. The "closed Kleisli category" keeps sequential composition (`>=>`); the monoidal product (`&&&`) is retired. CLAUDE.md load-bearing commitments + F#-feature-surface table + `AxiomTests.fs` H-004/H-005/H-006/H-010/H-062 entries updated in the same commit (the AXIOMS↔AxiomTests alignment discipline).

**Discipline reinforced.** This is the dual of "IR grows under evidence": *IR shrinks when the evidence never arrives.* A defer-with-trigger whose trigger fires and the consumer declines the surface is a deletion signal, not a keep signal. Future speculative algebra should carry the same deletion contract and be enforced at the next chapter close.

**Verification.** `Projection.Core` builds 0-warning under `TreatWarningsAsErrors`; the test project builds 0-warning after the `DiagnosticsTests` / `LineageTests` / `AxiomTests` surgery (the H-015 Lens-law tests rewritten from a `PassContext` carrier to a plain tuple, preserving the lens-law coverage since `Optics.fs` is otherwise untested); pure pool green. Net: ~716 Core LOC + ~2.5K test LOC removed. The ambiguous `SqlStorageType.to/ofPrimitiveType` (delete-as-dead vs promote-as-canonical) was **excluded** — it is an AR1/`ColumnType` decision, not an unambiguous deletion.

**Cross-references:** `AUDIT_2026_06_04_PERIPHERY_ADVERSARIAL.md` §8 (trajectory steelman), §2 (reclaim ledger); `CRYSTALLINE_FORM.md` §4 (do/do-not table) + Tranche-3 reachability; `AUDIT_2026_06_04_ACTIONABLES.md` Tranche 3; `DECISIONS 2026-05-22` (the retired trinity/duo/Kleisli-product commitments).

## 2026-06-04 — Registry drives the run (pass chain): one `chainSteps` source + the operator event-stream enrichment

**Decided (operator-outcomes work; the `/sidecar/projection` operator surface).** Two coupled changes to the operator-facing surface land together: (1) the pass-chain registry becomes the **single definition site that drives execution** — closing the metadata↔execution drift the prior "no parallel enumeration" claim only asserted; (2) the structured NDJSON event stream (`docs/logging-format.md`) is enriched from 5 emitted codes toward the §7 taxonomy.

**Registry drives the run (pass chain).** The Core pass chain was three hand-maintained parallel lists in `RegisteredTransforms.fs` — `all` (metadata), `allChainSteps` (execution), `allChainStepsFor policy profile` (execution-with-config) — in the same order, that had to agree by discipline. They had **already drifted**: `all` placed `TableRename` *before* the logical-emission passes; `allChainSteps` placed it *after* (so operator pins dominate). Resolution: one `chainSteps : ChainStep list` (the EXECUTION order, canonical) where `ChainStep = { Metadata : RegisteredTransformMetadata; Build : Policy -> Profile -> PassChainAdapter }` pairs each pass's classification surface with how it plugs into the run (lift strategy + `ComposeState` writeback + config threading). `all` = `chainSteps |> List.map _.Metadata` `@ StrategyRegistrations.all`; `allChainStepsFor` = `chainSteps |> List.map (ChainStep.build policy profile)`; `allChainSteps` = `allChainStepsFor Policy.empty Profile.empty` (byte-identical to the prior hand-written list). Both projections flow from one value, so the classification (`Sites`) rides the executing entity and `transform.registered` describes exactly what runs. `ChainStep` lives in `PassChainAdapter.fs` (after `Policy`/`Profile`/`ComposeState`/`TransformRegistry` in the Core compile order). **Supersedes the aspirational form** of the pillar-9 commitment "`RegisteredTransform<'In,'Out>` … single definition site; no parallel enumeration" — that was *true per pass* but *false across the chain* (the three lists); it is now true-by-construction for the pass chain. **Scope: pass chain only.** The emit phase (sibling-Π emitters) and the read adapter still execute via direct calls in `Compose.project`, registered as metadata but not registry-driven — the **E1–E4 full `registered ⇔ executed` isomorphism** is the named follow-up (design locked: `EmitStep { Metadata; Emit : EmitContext -> Outputs -> Outputs }` + an `emitSteps` single source folded over an `Outputs.seed`, mirroring `chainSteps`). That follow-up surfaced two real mismatches to resolve when it lands: `SuggestConfigEmitter` is executed-but-unregistered; `ConstraintFormatter`/`DacpacEmitter`/`StaticPopulationEmitter` are registered-but-run-in-other-contexts (render/dacpac/data).

**Event-stream enrichment (the keystone + Slices 1/2/4 + `transform.registered`).** `LogSink.fs` moved to the **top of the Pipeline compile order** (it depends only on `Projection.Core`), so the orchestration layer (`Compose`/`Deploy`/`FullExportRun`/CLI) can emit directly rather than only projecting post-hoc — this is the structural enabler, not a behavior change. On top of it: **Slice 1** projects the chain's accumulated writers (`composed.Trail`, `passEntries`, the DecisionSets) into `transform.applied`/`declined`/`lineage`/`diagnostic` (the §16 "boundary projects the writer" model — Core stays I/O-free); **Slice 2** emits live `extract`/`profile`/`emit` stage events + per-stage `summary.stageCompleted` via the new shared `LogSink.recordStageEvent`; **Slice 4** brackets every emitting verb (`emit`×3 / `skeleton` / `deploy` / `canary`) in a `withRun` envelope (`config.runStart` + mandatory terminal `summary.runComplete`), so the structured contract is no longer full-export-only; **`transform.registered`** (§7.4) emits the full registry inventory at run start carrying **per-Site** classification (`DataIntent` | `OperatorIntent<axis>`) rather than one lossy intent per transform — the pillar-9 totality surface made operator-visible, from the same registry that drives the chain. New `EventProjection.fs` holds the writer→envelope projections.

**Discipline.** Mirrors "IR grows under evidence" applied to enumerations: when two lists must agree by hand, collapse them to one source the moment a second consumer (here, execution) exists — drift is then structurally impossible, not disciplined-against. The `transform.registered` per-site shape improves on the §7.4 single-`intent` field (additive; the contract reserves additive extension).

**Verification.** `Projection.Core` + `Projection.Pipeline` + `Projection.Cli` build 0-warning under `TreatWarningsAsErrors`; registry/totality/pillar-9 suites 302/0; full pure pool green (no test was order-sensitive on the corrected `all` order). The E1–E4 emit/adapter isomorphism is byte-critical and Docker-canary-verified; it lands as its own staged diff on this committed base.

**Cross-references:** `docs/logging-format.md` §7 (code taxonomy), §10–§11 (runComplete + rollup), §16 (boundary projects the writer); `src/Projection.Core/RegisteredTransforms.fs` (`chainSteps`); `src/Projection.Core/PassChainAdapter.fs` (`ChainStep`); `src/Projection.Pipeline/EventProjection.fs`; `DECISIONS 2026-05-15 (late)` (pillar-9 registry as cross-cutting concern — the "single definition site" framing this makes true-by-construction for the pass chain).

## 2026-06-04 — Registry drives the run, all four contexts (E1–E4): the `registered ⇔ executed` isomorphism

**Decided (operator-outcomes work; extends the same-day pass-chain entry above).** The "registry drives the run" single-source discipline now spans every execution context, not just the pass chain. Each context's metadata (what `RegisteredAllTransforms.all` reads) and its execution (what the run dispatches) project from ONE value, so the registry describes exactly what runs. The climb surfaced **two real mismatches** — the entire point of the isomorphism — and resolved both.

**E1 — full-export emit phase.** The six sibling-Π emitters are one `Compose.emitSteps : EmitStep list` (`EmitStep = { Metadata; Emit : EmitContext -> Outputs -> Outputs }`). `projectFromChainWithState` folds them over an `Outputs.seed`; `RegisteredAllTransforms` projects their metadata from the same list. Steps are independent (no cross-`Outputs` reads — the SSDT step computes `decisionOverlay` + `policyConflicts` internally), so the fold is byte-identical to the prior hand-assembled `Outputs` (canary-verified: operator-reality round-trip + schema-migration + transfer canaries green; pure pool 2741/0). **Mismatch #1 (executed-but-unregistered): `SuggestConfigEmitter`** ran in the emit phase but was absent from the registry; the `emitSteps` single source now carries it (classified DataIntent).

**E2 — read adapter.** `CatalogReader.parse` + its metadata bind into one `Compose.readStep`; `Compose.read` / `readJson` dispatch through `readStep.Read`, and the registry reads `readStep.Metadata`. Behavior-neutral.

**E3 — transfer / data-load.** `Ingestion` / `DataLoadPlan` / `Transfer` / `Reconciliation` are all registered AND executed (in `TransferRun`); set-level isomorphic. Left executing at their dependency-ordered async sites (no parallel-list drift to collapse, unlike the pass chain); the isomorphism property test guards them.

**E4 — conditional emitters.** `DacpacEmitter` (→ `DockerImageEmitter`) and `ConstraintFormatter` (→ `Render`, `OperatorIntent Emission`) are registered + executed at their context-specific sites. **Mismatch #2 (registered-but-unexecuted): `StaticPopulationEmitter`** was registered `Active` but had ZERO production execution sites (only tests called `.statements`). Per its module header — the `InsertRow` realization for a fresh-empty target is the wide canary's data lane — it is now wired into the production CLI `canary` verb via `Deploy.schemaWithStaticPopulation` (the canonical schema-then-data deploy form: `SsdtDdlEmitter.statements` ++ `StaticPopulationEmitter.statements`). Identity to the prior schema-only canary when the source carries no static populations. **Resolution chosen over deletion** because the pre-deletion check contradicted the "dead/superseded" framing: it is a working, tested, *distinct* emitter (InsertRow vs `StaticSeedsEmitter`'s idempotent CDC-aware MERGE), so the "look before you delete" discipline applied — surfaced, then wired rather than removed. Verified: the production CLI canary is green with `emit.staticPopulation.statements` firing in the bench rollup.

**The keystone — the isomorphism is enforced, not just asserted.** `RegisteredAllTransformsBidirectionalTests.fs` carries the `registered ⇔ executed` property tests: every `emitStep` / `readStep` / `chainStep` executes through a transform present in `RegisteredAllTransforms.all` (no orphan execution); the emit phase is exactly six sibling-Π emitters each registered once (no orphan registration); `SuggestConfigEmitter` is registered (the E1 regression guard). A future transform wired to run without a registry entry — or an emit/read entry registered with no execution binding — fails the suite. This is the dual of the `IR grows under evidence` discipline applied to the execution surface: the registry cannot drift from the run because the run *is* the registry's projection.

**Discipline reinforced — "look before you delete" caught a near-miss.** The `StaticPopulationEmitter` decision was first taken as "delete (dead, superseded)"; the mandated pre-deletion check showed it was neither dead nor superseded (tested, distinct output algebra, named consumer in its header). Surfacing the contradiction — rather than executing the delete — turned a functionality-losing deletion into a correct wiring. Every "delete as dead" carries this check.

**Cross-references:** `src/Projection.Pipeline/Pipeline.fs` (`EmitStep` / `EmitContext` / `emitSteps` / `readStep`); `src/Projection.Pipeline/Deploy.fs` (`schemaWithStaticPopulation`); `src/Projection.Pipeline/RegisteredAllTransforms.fs` (derivation from the execution sources); `tests/Projection.Tests/RegisteredAllTransformsBidirectionalTests.fs` (the isomorphism property tests); the pass-chain entry immediately above (E0, the same-day predecessor).

## 2026-06-05 — The dynamic display: progressive disclosure (not a Voice register) serves the expert; every word is Apple-clear

**Decided (operator-led, dynamic-display build; reframes `INSTRUMENT_BACKLOG` slice 4).** Two corrections, both operator audibles, that bind how the display speaks and how it reveals depth.

**1. Progressive disclosure, not a Voice register, is the mechanism.** The slice-4 "Voice layer (plain ↔ expert *words*)" was a mis-derivation. THE_INSTRUMENT's thesis — *"the newcomer is the power user … Power is not a denser interface — it is fluency at the dig"* — is served by **velocity through depth**, never by swapping plain English for terse notation. The **expert is not the agent**; they are a human operator who carries the Phase-6 ontology and deserves the *same* calm, Apple-style ease as the newcomer. A jargon "expert mode" (e.g. `‖δ‖ = 14 · gate=… · exit 9` in place of the verdict) treats them as a machine parser and is **rejected** — there is no expert mode. The instrument presents the high-level topology and lets anyone **zoom into complexity on demand, one level per step, never the deep end.** The `Voice` type + `--voice` flag are dropped; the `Surface` abstraction (essence + dig) is kept as the seed of a depth-bearing node.

**The substrate (built this session): `View.Disclosure`.** A recursive `Disclosure of headline: string * Status * detail: View list` (Apple-HIG disclosure control), depth-aware in the renderer: the headline always shows; `View.writeToDepth depth` reveals `detail` to `depth` levels and collapses deeper nodes behind a `▸ N more` affordance (`Lane` is depth-aware likewise; `▸`/`▾` are glyph-paired Theme tokens). The calm default is **one level open** (operator-chosen). **Discriminating predicate:** `toJson` carries the full `detail` + `count` **regardless of render depth** — the machine lens never loses the tree the human collapsed (a naive "json mirrors the screen" drops the collapsed detail and fails). `--depth N|all` on `diff`; `--format json` is always the full tree. Two consumers: the changeset (move-lanes) + the gate (the formal proof under a `Details` disclosure). The Explore navigator (slice 6) is then "vary the depth budget along a cursor" — no new model. *Open follow-up (the glance, slice 5):* at `--depth 0` the move-lanes collapse but the `‖δ‖` panel still renders fully; fold it behind a disclosure when the topological glance lands.

**2. Apple-clear diction is a discipline** (`DYNAMIC_DISPLAY.md` §7.8). Every operator-facing word — verdict, affordance, action, disclosure headline — is written to the gold standard of **a content team that dwelled on the exact right words: targeted, concrete, simple, calm.** Banned: opaque / mystical wording (the operator's worked counterexamples — *"needs your nod"*, *"open a lane"*), drama (*"destroys structure"*), negation-framing (*"nothing destroyed"*), and **leaked internal identifiers** (a human reads `Country`, never `OS_KIND_Country`). The formal proof / notation (`‖δ‖`, exit codes, gate labels) is **the dig** — the essence made rigorous, reachable by anyone — never the essence itself.

**Cross-references:** `DYNAMIC_DISPLAY.md` §4 (the Voice→disclosure + diction forks) + §7 discipline 8; `src/Projection.Cli/View.fs` (`Disclosure`, `writeToDepth`, `defaultDepth`); `src/Projection.Cli/Surface.fs` (the kept essence+dig seed); `src/Projection.Cli/Theme.fs` (`collapsed`/`expanded`); `tests/Projection.Tests/ViewTests.fs` (the render-depth vs json-fidelity predicate); `INSTRUMENT_BACKLOG.md` §4 row 4.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-06` — The operator-facing voice: a twelve-rule register (evidential literalism) + the Event/Aggregate/Voice separation. (Amends the 2026-06 "Apple-clear diction is a discipline" entry.)**

Resolved across an operator-led session on the dynamic-display / voice branch. Three anchor docs now carry it: `THE_VOICE.md` (the register), `THE_STORYBOARD.md` (the surface, scene by scene), `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` (the build plan). The build has not started; this records what is settled before handoff.

**1. The register — authoritative, scientific, mature, humble; under evidential literalism.** The prior "Apple-clear diction" framing was *conversational* — possessive ("your data stayed put"), first-person ("I checked it matches"), warm ("It's yours"). That register is **retired**. The voice is now a precise measurement report a newcomer can read aloud, governed by one principle — **evidential literalism**: every sentence asserts a proposition grounded in its evidence, about a concretely-named subject, ordered by its true structure, with no rhetorical figure standing in for that precision. Operationalized as **twelve rules** (`THE_VOICE.md` §1): (1) no pronouns; (2) direction by imperative; (3) legible statement with the formal substantiation one level beneath; (4) verdicts asserted (the engine states what it proves; hedge only on genuine interpretation, e.g. a config recommendation); (5) the true verb (Drops/Deletes/Narrows — no euphemism, no drama); (6) gentle and direct, never colloquial; (7) neutral reference to the estate (never "your"); (8) ground every claim in its evidence (no "X, not Y" antithesis); (9) order by real structure (perfect aspect + explicit consequence); (10) name the exact referent; (11) concrete definite subjects; (12) stative, agentless voice.

**2. Rule 12 (stative, agentless voice) is the load-bearing refinement.** Removing the pronoun was insufficient: "Read the model — 300 tables" still narrates an implied actor. The pipeline reports *states and events*, never *actions it performed* — subject-led, passive or resultative, nominalizing a completed process ("Model read complete — 300 tables"). **Exception:** a gerund naming a live activity in progress is a state, not a deed ("Reading the model" is kept). Consequence: "self-check" was renamed **"round-trip verification"** (the "self" reintroduced the subject the register removes).

**3. The Event / Aggregate / Voice separation (the discipline; = integration-plan slice 0).** A site emits a *coded event* (a Code + typed payload, no prose) AND **declares its copy as a separable, harvested value** — the `TransformRegistry` pattern: declared at the site, harvested by a `Voice.all`, consumed by the renderer, held honest by a `code ⇔ copy` totality test. Inline prose welded to control flow (`sprintf` mid-fold) is **forbidden**. Voice is concern-shaped (a sibling of Lineage / Diagnostics / Bench / Registry) but has **no runtime write side** — the declarations are static copy data, projected at render.
- **Core-purity recommendation (reversible).** The event is emitted at the site (pure); the copy declaration lives as close as `F#-pure-core` allows — in-module for boundary / pipeline / CLI sites; a 1:1 projection-layer companion for pure-Core passes (`Voice.NullabilityPass ↔ NullabilityPass`). Typed move/gate `toView`s may sit beside their Core DUs under the `RemovalReason.toDiagnosticString` terminal-projection precedent. *Open: whether the declarations may live literally in the Core pass modules — a one-line flip, weighed only against F#-pure-core; settle in slice 1 and record it.*

**4. The catalog shape — hybrid.** Typed `toView` projections for the payload-shaped surfaces (the eleven moves, the gates, the proofs); a code-keyed declarative catalog for the flat high-volume codes (lifecycle / errors / config). Each mechanism where it fits.

**5. The live surface — streaming Watch from the start; defer the Diagnostics lift.** Per-stage live progress + ETA is in scope for the first build (raises slice-2 scope onto the `LogSink` envelope spine + stage codes + a streaming render path). The Diagnostics lift (a typed `DiagnosticPayload` DU replacing pass prose) is **deferred** behind a real consumer (slice 5), per IR-grows-under-evidence.

**6. "dig" retired from the voice vocabulary** → "the statement" / "the substantiation"; navigation reads "Show detail". The vision thesis ("one essence, infinitely diggable", `THE_INSTRUMENT.md`) is **kept** (operator call). The `Surface.fs` `essence` / `dig` code fields are the matching rename, **pending** the next time `Surface` is touched. `DECISIONS.md`'s prior diction entry is left as-recorded (append-only; its "dig" references describe `Surface.fs` as it was).

**7. The storyboard adds the concern-movement field as the surface's meta-shape** (`WAVE_6_MORPHOLOGY` §2): the emission axis (this run, integrated by `ChangeManifest`) × the episode axis (across runs, integrated by `LifecycleStore` / the FTC) × depth; plus a build-readiness map (LIVE / PARTIAL / LATENT, from the 2026-06-02 debrief) showing the engine already *proves* the migration end to end — what is unbuilt is almost entirely voice and surface (the streaming Watch, the record line, the timeline / ladder, the depth layer). The P-6 worked proof (`THE_STORYBOARD.md` §8) storyboards `migrate` frame by frame as the granularity target.

**Cross-references:** `THE_VOICE.md` (the twelve-rule register, lexicon, banned list); `THE_STORYBOARD.md` (nine acts · six streams · the concern-movement field · the build-readiness map · the P-6 worked proof); `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` (the slice sequence 0–5; §8 locked decisions); `HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` (the executing-agent letter). Amends the 2026-06 "Apple-clear diction is a discipline" entry (its conversational register, "the dig", and "the essence" are superseded). The integration plan's slice 0 (this discipline) is recorded here, so an executing agent starts at slice 1.

---

## 2026-06-07 — The CLI is four verbs over one `MovementSpec` (project / check / explain / seal)

**Decision.** The operator surface is re-envisioned from ~16 flat verbs (at
mixed altitudes — `skeleton` was a sub-mode sitting beside `transfer`, a whole
protein) to **four intents over one engine**: `project` (all data movement),
`check` (fidelity — canary / drift / data / ready), `explain` (understand —
diff / policy / node / suggest / migrate-preview), `seal` (provenance — eject /
approve). The emission-family verbs collapse into one typed `MovementSpec`
(`Destination × ModelSource × Scope × Strategy × DataOrigin × Baseline × Shape ×
Rekey × Reconcile × …`); `deploy` / `migrate` / `load` / `export` are one act —
`emit(B ⊖ A)` — distinguished only by the destination and the **auto-read
baseline A** (the engine reads the live state itself, so the operator never picks
deploy-vs-migrate; idempotent redeploy falls out as `‖B ⊖ A‖ = 0`).

**Why.** The verb sprawl was the morphology's "latent calculus" symptom — because
the delta-functor was never the *organizing principle of the surface*, each
protein grew its own verb with its own conditional tree. The interface question
is a **namespace of outcomes over the same input** (the protein catalog IS the
namespace), not the engine's algebra exposed; the orthogonality matrix is a red
herring at the interface (a namespace names only the wanted points). See
`THE_CLI.md` (the target design) + `THE_CLI_BACKLOG.md` (the shipped slices).

**Shape.** `Surface.parse` (Pipeline) turns argv into a typed `Intent`; the CLI
executors **delegate to the existing proven `run*` engine faces** (`Compose` /
`Deploy` / `MigrationRun` / `TransferRun` / `DriftRun` / `EjectRun` /
`DataIntegrityChecker` / `PolicyDiff` / `FullExportRun`), so exit codes and
behavior are preserved *by construction*. Targets + the default model are named
in `projection.json` (or `PROJECTION_CONFIG`); a target's `conn` is a `env:` /
`file:` **reference**, never a literal string (D9 holds in config). Config holds
addressing + benign defaults only; intent/danger (`--go`, `--allow-drops`,
`--rekey`) stay on the command line. The legacy 16-verb dispatch + the Argu DUs
(`FullExportArgs` / `TransferArgs` / `VerifyDataArgs` / `VerbArgs`) + the Argu
package are **removed** (deprecate, don't shim).

**Honored vs noted.** `--scope data` routes to the DML-only transfer; `--scope
schema` skips the data leg; `--reconcile` threads to the re-key. `--how` /
`--from` / `--data synthetic|none` are **accepted but surfaced as a named note**
(`noteUnhonored`) — the current engine emits its default; wiring those knobs is
an evidence-gated follow-up (no silent drop). Consciously dropped (named):
`full-export --mute-category` / `--debug`; the `emit --config` `[accepted]`
console narration (the data rides the NDJSON stream).

**Witness.** `MovementSurfaceTests` (21 pure tests); pure pool 2829 / 0;
runtime smoke (help, routing, aliasing, D9 refusal, exit codes). Surface types:
`src/Projection.Pipeline/MovementSpec.fs` + `MovementSurface.fs`; executors:
`src/Projection.Cli/Program.fs`.

---

## 2026-06-07 — The perf gate runs warm-only; auto-warming from the docker-probe is rejected (zombie risk)

**Decision.** `scripts/perf-gate.sh` gains a **warm-only precondition**: it gates
only when a warm SQL Server is reachable (the `projection-mssql-warm` container
is running, or `PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR` is set); otherwise it soft-skips
(exit 0). The baseline is recorded warm; a cold-start run (ephemeral container +
~2GB image pull) inflates every timing label 2-10× and **false-trips the gate
while the canary stays GREEN** — the recurring "REGRESSION in N labels" the
operator kept seeing.

**Root cause (diagnosed).** `session-start.sh` warms the container only if Docker
is ready inside its 20s window; when Docker comes up later (the `docker-probe.sh`
auto-repair brings up only the *daemon*), the warm container is never
established, so the Stop-hook gate ran the canary cold. The hard gate's trippers
were borderline CPU-time labels (≥50ms) under cold-start + 4-core contention —
**no count/volume label ever regressed** (those are deterministic), confirming
it was never a code regression.

**Rejected alternative — lazy warm-kick from `docker-probe.sh`.** An attempt to
have the probe kick `warm-sql.sh start` in the background once Docker is up was
**reverted**: on the no-swap host the capped SQL container still OOM-died
(`Exited 255`), leaving a **zombie warm container** that poisons the
warm-honoring test runs (`test.sh docker/canary/focus` + `EphemeralContainer
Fixture` trust `PROJECTION_MSSQL_CONN_STR` / a running `projection-mssql-warm`)
— it manifested as 30 `SqlException` failures in the pure pool. Forcing a warm
container on a host that can't keep one alive is net-negative; warm
establishment stays owned by `session-start.sh` + `warm-sql.sh` (which
health-check + memory-cap), and the gate now **degrades gracefully** (skip, not
false-trip) when warm isn't available. Re-baselining was also rejected — the
baseline is machine-shared; recording this container's variable cold timings
would be wrong for CI/dev.

**Witness.** Offline replay of the latest canary snapshot against the baseline:
0 hard regressions (the only over-threshold labels are `readside.*`, already
I/O-soft). Pure pool returned to 2829 / 0 after removing the zombie container.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-06 (later)` — Voice slice 1 landed: the Voice seam + the `code ⇔ copy` totality test; the two open sub-calls resolved (Core-purity → 1:1 projection-layer companion; the `Surface.fs` rename).**

Slice 1 of `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` §7 is built (plus the slice-2 stage scaffold), green on the full pure pool. What landed and what is now settled:

**1. The Voice seam exists (hybrid §5, mechanism 2).** `src/Projection.Cli/Voice.fs` is the code-keyed declarative catalog `Code → operator copy` for the flat LIVE lifecycle / verdict / config codes — the boundary-side sibling of the engine's ubiquitous language. Each entry is a separable declarative `Copy` value (statement-first `Surface` built from the envelope payload, tagged with its `THE_VOICE.md` section), harvested into `Voice.all`; `TtyRenderer.buildSummaryView` now looks the verdict line up **by code** (`canary.diffEmpty`/`canary.divergence` §6, else `summary.runComplete` §3) rather than authoring `"SUCCEEDED"`/`"green"` inline. Codes voiced: the spine (`config.runStart`, `config.connectionResolved`, `summary.runComplete`, `config.validationFailed`) + the §13 Watch stage stream (`extract`/`profile`/`emit`.`started`/`.completed`, `summary.stageCompleted`) + the verdict proofs (`canary.*`). The open-ended `pipeline.config.*` / connection / permission error space is voiced by a routing **`errorFrame`** keyed on the code prefix (§10/§14), not one entry per `sprintf` subcode.

**2. The `code ⇔ copy` totality test (the sibling of `registered ⇔ executed`).** `tests/Projection.Tests/VoiceTotalityTests.fs` holds the copy honest by construction: every in-scope LIVE code has a `Voice.all` entry and every entry maps to an in-scope code (bidirectional); no entry voices a code the engine cannot emit (⊆ the known-emittable inventory); every entry cites a recognized `THE_VOICE.md` §; the `stageName` map is operator-shaped (never the internal verb) over the emitted stages; `errorFrame` routing is total. **The register is itself a test:** a mechanical banned-list guard (`THE_VOICE.md` §1 + §2.2) scans every voiced surface (filled + empty payload) and every error frame for pronouns / the antithesis tic / euphemism / drama / figurative terms / colloquialism / system-shout — a line that breaks a rule fails the build.

**3. Core-purity sub-call — RESOLVED: the 1:1 projection-layer companion** (the handoff's recommendation; operator-confirmed 2026-06-06). Per-site Voice copy for **pure-Core passes** lives in a `Voice.<Pass> ↔ <Pass>` companion in the projection/CLI layer, **never** in `Projection.Core` — the F#-pure-core / no-prose-in-Core commitment holds unbroken. **Load-bearing constraint that forces this:** the `View` / `Surface` substrate lives in the downstream `Projection.Cli` project, so `Projection.Core` *cannot* reference it (dependency direction) — copy that produces a `View`/`Surface` value structurally cannot live in a Core module. "Declarations literally in Core" would therefore mean a Core-visible *structured-string* representation projected to `View` at the CLI boundary; the companion is cleaner and is the chosen form. No companion is needed yet: the move/gate/proof carriers are all Pipeline/CLI or boundary-projected (`EventProjection`); the pure-Core pass lift is the deferred slice-5 `DiagnosticEntry.Message` work (§8 decision 4), which adopts this companion shape.

**4. The `Surface.fs` rename — DONE.** `Surface.Essence` → `Surface.Statement`, `Surface.Dig` → `Surface.Substantiation` (and `Comparison.catalogEssence` → `Comparison.catalogStatement`), aligning the code vocabulary with the voice docs (§1 rule 3 — "legible statement, formal substantiation beneath"; "dig" retired). The diff-surface *copy strings* (`Comparison`, Act 2) are unchanged — they are payload-shaped Act-2 move copy, voiced to the register in a later slice (mechanism 1); this rename only realigned the field vocabulary, per the "land it when you next touch `Surface`" instruction.

**5. What remains (the rest of the slice sequence).** Slice 2 (the streaming Watch render path + unifying `MigrationRun`/`TransferRun`/`EjectRun` onto the `LogSink` envelope spine — only the **stage-code vocabulary** is scaffolded so far); slice 3 (aggregate-at-scale §12 + the timeline/ladder §8); slice 4 (lift `configError`/`ValidationError` copy + route `printErrors` through `Voice.errorSurface` + catch-and-translate exceptions + voice Argu `Usage`); slice 5 (the deferred typed `DiagnosticPayload` lift); and the cross-cutting mechanism-1 typed `toView`s for the §4 moves / §5 gates / §6 proofs (beside their `EventProjection` boundary projections; `TtyRenderer.buildGateSurface` is the existing model). Research maps for slices 2–4 (per-run stage→§13 mapping, the LogSink-unification gap with file:line, the §12/§8 accessor gaps, the error/config lift inventory, the mechanism-1 landing map, and the full test blast radius) were produced this session by a five-agent research pass and are captured in detail in **`THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md`** — the file:line execution layer beneath `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md`. Read it before opening any of slices 2–5.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-06 (later, slice 2)` — The streaming Watch (the live run, §13) lands over the already-emitted stage stream, with a minimum-dwell floor on the perceptual surface.**

The live-run surface (`THE_VOICE.md` §13 / `THE_STORYBOARD.md` Act 4) is built as `src/Projection.Cli/Watch.fs` — a stage board that fills in as the run happens, opt-in via `--watch` on `full-export` (a real TTY required). Settled this slice:

**1. Watch is a *rendering* of the LogSink stage stream, never a second emit surface.** The board subscribes to the existing envelopes (`LogSink.addSubscriber`) — exactly what channel 1 writes, after the verbosity/mute filter — so the live surface and the NDJSON can never drift (the "one substrate, two lenses" discipline carried to the live leg). This slice voices the stage events `Compose.runWithConfig` **already** emits (`extract/profile/emit.started`, `summary.stageCompleted`); no new events (IR grows under evidence). The board reuses the §13 stage copy already in `Voice.all` (the gerund-in-progress while `Active`; the resultative + measured duration on `Done`).

**2. The minimum-dwell floor (operator concern; the load-bearing perceptual decision).** In a synchronous run the only live events are the discrete stage transitions, so a fast stage would flip `started → completed` faster than the eye can read. The floor is a **minimum inter-frame interval**: each visible board frame is held ≥ the floor before the next is shown. Crucially it adds **only the remainder below the floor** — a stage that already ran longer than the floor is never delayed (`Watch.dwellMs floor lastRenderAt now = max 0 (floor − (now − lastRenderAt))`) — so the calm is bought by not flickering past a frame, *not* by slowing the run. **Stage granularity only:** the stream stays at the level worth representing as a stream (the handful of acts), not sub-stage micro-events that would themselves flicker. Default `120 ms`, overridable via `PROJECTION_WATCH_DWELL_MS` for perceptual tuning without a rebuild. The floor + board are pure + clock-injected, so the perceptual contract is unit-tested (`WatchTests`) without a TTY or a real sleep; the Spectre `Live` shell is the thin boundary.

**3. Scope + the deferred run-unification.** Wired to `full-export` only — the one run that emits the stage stream today. Unifying `MigrationRun`/`TransferRun`/`EjectRun` onto the `LogSink` envelope spine (so they too stream), plus intra-stage progress + ETA, is the rest of slice 2 (mapped at `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` §4.2/§4.4). **Scope assumption recorded:** the dwell is enforced by the subscriber sleeping the emitting thread, which briefly holds the `LogSink` lock — safe because the run is synchronous + single-threaded and channel 1 is nulled under `--watch` (no contention). A future concurrent/async emitter moves the dwell to a drain loop on a render thread (named in the Watch module header + build map §4.3). Default behavior is unchanged — `--watch` is opt-in.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-07` — Voice slice 4 (errors as voice) + mechanism-1 gates (§5 voiced over the closed `GateLabel` DU).**

Two voice increments landed green:

**1. Errors as voice (slice 4 core).** `Voice.errorsSurface : ValidationError list -> Surface` frames a refusal by its dominant (first) error through the §10/§14 `errorFrame` routing, lists every located cause beneath (rule 3 — the statement is legible, the codes ride in the substantiation, never on the lead). `TtyRenderer.renderErrors` renders it to stderr; `full-export`'s `ConfigInvalid` / `RunFailed` paths now route through it instead of `Console.Error.WriteLine "failed:"` + `[code] message`. **Codes are unchanged** — the structured `config.validationFailed` NDJSON stays the machine channel; only the operator surface is voiced. **Deferred (the long tail):** lifting the ~30 `configError` `sprintf` messages into the catalog, migrating the ~48 `printErrors` call sites, voicing the Argu `Usage` strings, and the `ReadSide.fs:965` catch-and-translate — all mapped in `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` §6.1; the message text is already located/concrete, so the frame surfaces it correctly without the per-message lift.

**2. The §5 gates voiced (mechanism 1).** `Voice.gateStatement : Preflight.GateLabel -> Status × statement × action option` is the typed projection over the **closed** gate DU — the gate⇔copy totality is the closed-DU analog of code⇔copy (a `VoiceTotalityTests` test fails if a `GateLabel` variant is added without §5 copy). All eight labels now carry register-correct copy (the consequence as meaning + a plain active imperative — `Approve · Grant · Map · Check · Correct · Allow · halt`), where before only `UndeclaredDestructiveChange` got distinct prose and the other seven collapsed to the bare axis name. `TtyRenderer.buildGateSurface` delegates to `Voice.gateSurface`. **Register correction caught by the banned-list guard:** the prior "<command> refused —" gate framing used "refused" (a system-shout lean); a gate *stops to ask* (§5), so the surface now leads directly with the consequence (the command context lives in the surrounding CLI narration). The true verb holds — "This change **drops** a database object" (§5 rule 5), not the prior "destroys structure" (drama).

**Still pending of "all five":** the §4-move / §6-proof typed `toView`s (mechanism 1, the migrate-preview + canary/verify surfaces — `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` §6.2); slice 3 (aggregate-at-scale §12 + timeline/ladder §8); the slice-2 run-unification (Migration/Transfer/Eject onto the spine) + intra-stage ETA; slice 5 (the typed `DiagnosticPayload` lift — **deferred by design** behind a real consumer per IR-grows-under-evidence; the integration plan §8 decision 4 marks it optional/last). Pure pool green at each step.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-07 (later)` — Reconciling the voice work with the four-verb CLI re-envisioning; merged main, took its `Program.fs` verbatim.**

The CLI was re-envisioned on `main` (`THE_CLI.md`, PR #583) — the ~16 verbs collapsed into **four** (`project` / `check` / `explain` / `seal`) over one `MovementSpec` (`Projection.Pipeline/MovementSpec.fs` + `MovementSurface.fs`), with the 16-verb Argu dispatch removed. This landed in parallel with the voice slices. The reconciliation:

1. **Take main on the CLI surface.** Merged `origin/main`; the only real conflict was `Program.fs` (main rewrote it; a slice-4 voice-error hunk auto-merged into a non-overlapping region). Per the operator's direction ("we're taking main on this one"), `Program.fs` is reset to main's exact version — the voice-error wiring does not sneak in via the merge.
2. **The voice machinery is unchanged and survives.** Main kept and rewired `Voice.fs`, `Watch.fs` (now `--watch` on `project --to <folder> --config`), `TtyRenderer.fs`, `View.fs`, `Surface.fs`, the `LogSink` event spine, `EventProjection`, `Config`, `CatalogDiff`, `Episode`/`RunLedger`. The slice-1/2/4 voice surfaces (`Voice.lookup`/`errorsSurface`/`gateSurface`, `Watch`, `renderErrors`) all build green against main's `Program.fs` and stay test-covered (`VoiceTotalityTests`, `WatchTests`, `TtyRendererTests`).
3. **`THE_CLI.md` is a sibling, not a contradiction.** It writes in the `THE_VOICE.md` register (its §5 preview/refusal footers are voice-correct) and declares itself a sibling of the voice docs. The voice is the *register*; `THE_CLI` is the *surface* it speaks on.
4. **Voice surfaces are BUILT-but-not-wired into the new executors.** `errorsSurface`/`gateSurface`/`renderErrors` exist + tested but main's `Program.fs` still uses plain `printErrors` / inline refusal prose. Wiring them into the four executors (replacing those) is a **deliberate follow-up** with the team's CLI direction — not snuck in via merge.
5. **Docs refactored toward the new surface.** `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` gains a **§0.5 reconciliation** (the old verb → new face map; what's superseded — every `Program.fs` file:line ref, the run-type dispatch framing, the now-obsolete "voice Argu `Usage`" since the Argu files are deleted — vs. what stays accurate — every Pipeline/Core file:line, since main didn't touch them); the status table is updated (run-unification **subsumed** by `MovementSpec`; the §6 proofs now home on `check`, the §4 moves on `explain`). `THE_VOICE_INTEGRATION.md` gains a forward-pointer noting its §3.1 run types are now engine faces behind `MovementSpec`.

The CLI moved from sixteen verbs to four; the voice's substance (the seam, the `code ⇔ copy` totality, the dwell floor, the §12/§8 rendering, the mechanism-1 `toView`s) is unchanged — only the dispatch surface it renders on moved.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-07 (errors wired)` — The voice error surface is wired into the four-verb CLI: `printErrors` renders the §10/§14 register; the `migrate` `REFUSED` shouts are revoiced.**

Following the reconciliation (`2026-06-07 (later)`), the voice error surface is now live on the new surface — the convergence `THE_CLI.md` §5 asked for:

1. **`printErrors` → the voiced §10/§14 surface (one change, total coverage).** `Program.fs`'s `printErrors` (the ~30-call-site helper every executor's error path routes through) now delegates to `TtyRenderer.renderErrorsTo` (the `Voice.errorsSurface` projection): a plain statement, the located cause + the code in the **substantiation** (never the code on the lead line), and the next move. This fixes the §10 violation (`  [<code>] <message>` led with the code) across `project` / `check` / `explain` / `seal` in one edit, no per-site churn. The prior `printErrorLine` is retired. **Codes unchanged** — the `config.validationFailed` / `transfer.*` NDJSON is the machine channel, untouched; only the operator copy moved to the register. Smoke-tested end-to-end (a `pipeline.config.fileNotFound` renders `✕ The configuration has a problem…` / `problem …` / `code …` / `→ Correct…`).

2. **The six `migrate` `REFUSED` system-shouts revoiced (§2.2 banned-word fix).** `reportPreviewOutcome` + `reportMigrationError` led their refusals with `REFUSED —` (a system-shout, banned). Each now leads with the consequence as meaning and keeps the imperative next move (e.g. `%d destructive change(s) are undeclared. Re-run with --allow-drops …`), per §5. Surgical string fixes; no structural change to the refusal renderers.

3. **Still deliberate-follow-up (the §5 safety surface).** Routing `reportPreviewOutcome` (the migrate/project preview), `reportMigrationError`, and the connection/permission/tightening pre-flight renderers through `Voice.gateSurface`/a voiced preview footer is **not** done here — those are payload-shaped (`MigrationError` / `GateRefusal` / `MigrationPreview`) and load-bearing for `THE_CLI.md` §5's preview-by-default safety model; rewiring them is the mechanism-1 §5 step, done carefully rather than via a blanket helper. The `Voice.gateSurface` projection (all 8 `GateLabel`s) is built + tested, ready to wire.

Pure pool green (2878 passed). No test asserts on the CLI's human error/refusal prose (verified), so the register change is test-safe.

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08` — The masterwork voice backlog (`THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md`) + the forward-looking handoff update, grounded against the flows surface.**

The voice machinery is built (slices 1/2/4 + the gate/error surfaces, all totality-tested) and the CLI has re-grounded twice (16 verbs → 4 verbs → the **flows** surface, `projection <flow>`, dispatched via `runPlan` over `PlanAction`). To let a new agent iterate on *deeply incorporating the voice into the rendered TTY*, two artifacts:

1. **`THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md` (new — the masterwork execution backlog).** Built from a full render-surface inventory pass over the flows-surface `Program.fs` (1876 lines): every operator-facing output site classified VOICED vs RAW, each raw site mapped to its storyboard act (0–8) + `THE_VOICE.md` section (§3/§4/§5/§6/§9/§10/§13/§14), the natural renderer named, ordered into seven waves. **Headline finding:** the machinery is whole but mostly unwired — only six sink families render in register; `Voice.gateSurface`/`TtyRenderer.renderGate` are total over the closed `Preflight.GateLabel` DU, banned-list-tested, and **have zero callers**. Wave order: **Wave 0** kills the four live `%A` raw-DU dumps (`Program.fs:1167,1279,575/579,1752`) + four system-shout leads (`"canary RED"`, `"DRIFT DETECTED"`, `"verification FAILED"`, `"FAILED self-verification"`) — the §2.2 breaches live today; **Wave 1** wires the idle `renderGate` into every pre-flight refusal (zero new infra, highest leverage); **Waves 2–6** the §9 ‖δ‖ preview (reusing `Comparison.renderCatalogDiff`, proven by `runDiff`), the §6 proofs, the §13 success narration + redundant-header cleanup, the §4 move surfaces, the synthetic surface. It supersedes `THE_VOICE_BUILD_MAP.md` §6 (the execution layer — that map predates both CLI re-envisionings) while referencing its still-accurate architecture (§1), code inventory (§2), and test blast-radius (§8).

2. **`HANDOFF_VOICE_2026_06_06.md` updated (append-only).** A 2026-06-08 forward-looking addendum (a letter, not a status report) prepended with a "read this first" banner and appended in full: what changed since the original (machinery built; CLI re-envisioned twice to the flows surface; errors/gates partly wired), the new backlog as the map, the wave order (Wave 0 then Wave 1 first), and the binding disciplines. The original anchor-set letter is preserved verbatim as the register orientation it still is.

**One open sub-decision named, not yet resolved (recorded for the executing agent to settle in Wave 1):** the `--go`/`PROJECTION_ALLOW_EXECUTE` intent gate has no `Preflight.GateLabel` variant. Recommendation: voice it through a flat `gate.intent` `errorSurface` code (a CLI-surface concern, not an engine pre-flight) rather than adding a closed-DU variant — keeps the engine's gate DU about engine pre-flights. To be confirmed when Wave 1 lands.

No code or events changed — this is documentation grounding the rendering work. Tree green (build + 2870 pure) at the prior merge (`9bae06e`).

---

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (later)` — The voice backlog re-grounded against the North Star: vision first, mechanics subordinate.**

The first cut of `THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md` read as a mechanical punch list — file:line fixes with no North Star, the failure mode being that an agent initialized on it would produce banned-list-clean strings that miss the soul. Re-authored to lead with *why*, ingesting the supporting docs so they speak one language:

1. **A new §0 "The North Star."** Frames the whole work against `NORTH_STAR.md` (the engine is one adjunction `Ingest ∘ Project = id`; fidelity is a *theorem the engine proves about itself*, not a property a human verifies) and `THE_INSTRUMENT.md` (the three layers Structure/Style/**Voice**; one essence infinitely diggable; the newcomer is the power user; *the instrument disappears*). The Voice is named as the **third layer** — the one that renders the theorem as plain human confidence. The load-bearing reframe: **every raw render site is a seam where the instrument fails to disappear** — the operator sees the apparatus (`%A`, `norm=`, `REFUSED`, a leaked exit code) instead of the truth. A `%A` at `:1167` is not a lint finding; it is the migration's *verdict* rendered as an internal DU dump.

2. **A new §1 "What 'done' means (the bar the guard cannot see)."** The `VoiceTotalityTests` banned-list guard is necessary, not sufficient — it catches a pronoun/shout/`%A` but cannot tell whether a line is true, grounded, or kind. The bar: one register two velocities (§0); essence on top, proof one level beneath (§3/§6/§10); grounded authority (rule 4/8); ends on the move; the instrument disappears. **The felt arc (§7) is the target, not the string.** The acceptance question for every item is "would the newcomer trust it on sight, would the master read it as a glance, and does the instrument disappear behind it?" — not "does the guard pass?".

3. **Each wave reframed by the felt-arc moment it restores**, not just the §2.2 break it fixes: Wave 1 (gates) is *the consent moment — trust earned in plain words*; Wave 2 (‖δ‖) is *the soul, said plain — minimality is the faithfulness proof, today rendered as `norm=`*; Wave 3 (proofs) is *"This is the soul"*; Wave 4 (§13) is *the rhythm names the next move*. The file:line mechanics are preserved in full beneath each — vision-led, mechanics-subordinate.

4. **The handoff updated** with a "Read the *why* before you touch a string" section: the mandated reading order (`NORTH_STAR` → `THE_INSTRUMENT` → `THE_VOICE` §0/§6/§7/§11/§15) and the explicit warning that initializing on the file:line list alone produces guard-clean strings that miss the soul.

No code changed — documentation grounding the rendering work in the vision. The mechanical inventory (the render-site classification, the renderer-per-gap, the wave order) is unchanged and intact; it now sits under the North Star rather than standing in for it.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (register-grounded)` — THE_VOICE.md made operable in the backlog; the handoff boiled down.**

Two refinements so an executing agent cannot drift, and orients fast:

1. **The register is now in-doc and per-wave (`THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md`).** A new §2 "The register in one screen" carries the twelve rules (§1), the banned list (§2.2), and the off→on calibration table (§11) inline — framed as "the corrections you will otherwise re-introduce by instinct" — with the standing instruction to keep `THE_VOICE.md` open, the distillation being no substitute for the source. Every wave (0–6) now carries a **Today → In register** worked example: the actual raw string at the site and the gold-standard voiced line derived from `THE_VOICE.md`, with the rule/section it satisfies (e.g. Wave 1's example is literally §11's worst case — `migrate refused — undeclared destructive change · exit 9` → `The IX_Order_Stale index will be dropped. No data is lost, but the drop is not auto-reversible. → Approve the removal, or halt.`). The target string shape is unambiguous at each site, so producing a guard-clean-but-soulless line is no longer the path of least resistance.

2. **The handoff is distilled.** The verbose addendum is replaced by "The whole thing in six lines" (machinery built / map is the backlog / looks mechanical and isn't / read why first / the bar is not the guard / start Wave 0 then Wave 1), then a one-paragraph machinery inventory and a short "lines that bind." Same content, a fraction of the read.

No code changed. The mechanical inventory is intact; it now sits under the North Star and inside the register, with worked examples making the register operable at every site.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (legibility axiom)` — The substantiation translates too: algebra/erudite jargon out, domain technicality in; the bar is the technical layperson.**

A register clarification the worked examples forced into the open. The first cut of the backlog's `Today → In register` examples put arcane tokens in the deeper `beneath:` substantiation lines (`‖δ‖ = 312 = CDC capture count`, `residual ∅ · Ingest ∘ Project = id`, `CDC = 0 · zero ALTERs`, `residual ≠ ∅`, `π ∘ σ ≈ id`). This is **axiomatically wrong** by `THE_VOICE.md` §2.1 ("The boundary translates — *always*") and the §11 calibration `‖δ‖ norm ▲ 3` → `Total changes: 3`, which holds *at every depth*, not just on the lead. The substantiation is the statement **made rigorous in plain words**, never the statement trailed by its glyphs — the depth is more plain precise detail, not a descent into notation.

The governing bar, stated for execution: **every rendered string — statement and substantiation alike — must be legible to a technical layperson** (a developer or DBA who knows databases but nothing of this engine's internals). The line, sharpened with the operator: **algebra and erudite jargon are out; domain concreteness and purposeful technicality are in.**
- **Out (never rendered):** `‖δ‖ ∅ ∘ ≈`, norm, residual, commuting square, isomorphism, torsor, quotient, surrogate, `Ingest ∘ Project` — recast in plain/domain terms (rows changed · nothing left over · the database matches the model · one difference remains).
- **In (stays — sharpens, not obscures):** CDC, ALTER/ADD/DROP, idempotent, index, column, schema, the exit code. A DBA knows them; they are concrete and purposeful, not category theory.

Captured in the backlog as: §1's bar sentence; a §2 "axiom, stated for execution" callout with an algebra→plain table and an explicit "stays" list; §1 rule-2 rewritten ("the proof is plain too"); every `beneath:` example purged of algebra (the §6 gold statements — including "Confirmed idempotent…" — restored, since idempotent/CDC/ALTER are permitted); and the one surviving formula (the §0 adjunction law) explicitly tagged "the one piece of algebra in this document… never rendered to the operator; the thing the Voice translates *from*." Verified mechanically: no algebra token appears in any rendered ✓ example or `beneath:` line. No code changed.

**`DECISIONS 2026-06-08 (full-zoom surfaces)` — Appendix A: the finished surfaces at whole-screen fidelity.**

The per-wave examples were *lines*; the next agent needs to see the *whole screen*. Added Appendix A to `THE_VOICE_BACKLOG.md` — six fully-rendered operator surfaces at increasing zoom, grounded in `THE_STORYBOARD.md` §3 (the act cards) + §8 (the P-6 frame-by-frame), as the producer's reference for "what done looks like":

1. **A.1 The migrate preview (acts 2→3) at three zooms** — the essence (verdict + big rocks + next move), the same surface with one node dug in (`▸ show detail`), and at scale (2,140 changes — same one-line verdict, cluster-by-table, cap-and-name).
2. **A.2 The gate in full** (declared-loss + data-compat) — consequence as meaning, one lever, plain imperatives, the affordance keys.
3. **A.3 The live run (Watch) frame by frame** — in-flight (gerund stages + honest ETA), done-and-verified, the no-op edge, and a mid-run failure with the safe-state line.
4. **A.4 The proofs essence-then-dig** — verified (plain), the same surface dug in (the depth is *more plain detail*, never notation), and the divergence (the P-9 screen).
5. **A.5 The timeline + ladder** — the streak, the named failed run, the one lever; plus the genesis edge.
6. **A.6 Arrival/setup** — the readback, and the three config registers (required-missing / optional-unset / set-but-invalid), all calm.

Two disciplines made explicit in the appendix: (a) every surface uses the `Theme` glyph tokens with color always riding a glyph (NO_COLOR-safe); (b) **the legibility axiom holds at every depth** — where `THE_STORYBOARD.md` §8's illustrative substantiation still shows algebra (`‖δ‖ = 312`, `residual ∅`), these surfaces render the plain form the operator reads (e.g. "the same 312 the database's change-tracking recorded as changed"; "the read-back is identical to the model — nothing left over"). Mechanically verified: no algebra token appears in any rendered surface line; the only machine token beneath is the exit code or the diagnostic code. No code changed.
